title: Astronomica
caption: Adapted from an early attempt at a stutter house track and then taken in a different direction
about: Penned somewhere between 14 and 40 AD, Marcus Manilius’s Astronomica acts as the Western world’s debut concept album on the cosmos—and stands as our earliest surviving comprehensive treatise on astrology. Spread across five sweeping "books" of Latin didactic poetry, the work completely ignores the modern divide between science and mysticism. Instead, Manilius flawlessly mixes the two, treating the cold, hard math of celestial geometry as the necessary baseline to decode the stars' divine influence on our lives. Heavy with Stoic philosophy, this epic paints the universe not as a random void, but as a single, breathing organism perfectly orchestrated by immutable fate and divine reason (ratio).
lyrics:
The sky tells us everything
The sky tells us everything

Circles within circles
Every motion by design,
Eyes trackin' orbits 
In a sky so divine.
Heaven sets the tempo 
While our world stays confined.
As the sky moves above us 
With stories older than time.
And then I move off-beat, 
Chasing feelings I can’t see.

Astronomica
Astronomica
Astronomica
Astronomica
Celestial
Phenomena

Maiden draws the patient heart,
Lion sparks the blazing start,
Twins divide the restless part,
Water-bearers flood the dark.
Threads of fire, threads of air,
Shape how we hope and care;
Not our fate, but how we fare
All the signs are right up there.
Every star’s a mirror 
Sending signals back to me,
Explaining my emotions,
As universal symmetry.

Astronomica

Astronomica
Astronomica
Celestial
Phenomena

The cosmos spin,
But minds stay right
We seize the day
By watching the night.
Fortune turns,
But we still choose
What we win and what we lose.
The stars don’t bind
Or cast our line
They only reflect
Our space and time.

If I learn your language 
If I play your game
If I map the heavens
Will it all seem the same?
